cards.definition: A noun is a person, place, or thing (e.g., hotel, playa, amigo).
All Spanish nouns are either masculine (el) or feminine (la).
Nouns ending in -o are usually masculine, and nouns ending in -a are usually feminine.
A pronoun replaces a noun (yo, tú, él, nosotros, ellos).
A verb describes an action or state (comer, dormir, ser).
Conjugation is changing a verb form depending on the subject.
Because verb endings already indicate the subject.
A word that describes a noun (bonito, alto, interesante).
Adjectives must match the noun in gender and number.
El chico alto vs. la chica alta vs. los chicos altos.
Use SER for identity, origin, permanent traits, and general characteristics.
Use ESTAR for temporary states, emotions, physical conditions, and location.
Estoy cansado. = I am tired.

cards.term: Estar conjugation (yo, tú, él, nosotros, ellos)
cards.definition: Estoy, estás, está, estamos, están.
Use MUY before adjectives or adverbs: muy dramático, muy bien.
Use mucho/a/os/as with nouns or as an adverb with verbs: muchas personas, duermo mucho.
MUY describes qualities; MUCHO describes quantity or degree.
They are verbs where the stem vowel changes in certain conjugations.
Pensar → pienso.
Dormir → duermo.
Pedir → pido.
Stem changes do not happen in the nosotros form in the present tense.
Decir → digo.
romantic | La película es romántica. | The movie is romantic. | Adjective; changes for gender and number.
talented | Mi artista favorita es muy talentosa. | My favorite artist is very talented. | Adjective agreement applies.
lively | El concierto es muy animado. | The concert is very lively. | Often used for people, events, or places.
artistic | Ella es artística. | She is artistic. | Adjective; match gender.
dramatic | Los narradores son muy dramáticos. | The announcers are very dramatic. | Use muy before adjectives.
fun | La fiesta es divertida. | The party is fun. | Adjective; not the same as the verb divertir.
boring | Los partidos son aburridos. | The games are boring. | Adjective agreement matters.
aggressive | El personaje es agresivo. | The character is aggressive. | Adjective; changes with gender.
misogynistic | Las letras son misóginas. | The lyrics are misogynistic. | Adjective; less common but important for discussion.
positive | El mensaje es positivo. | The message is positive. | Adjective agreement.
negative | El mensaje es negativo. | The message is negative. | Adjective agreement.
catchy | La canción es pegajosa. | The song is catchy. | Often used for songs and music.
problematic | Los narcocorridos son problemáticos. | Narcocorridos are problematic. | Adjective agreement.
violence | Hay mucha violencia en esa música. | There is a lot of violence in that music. | Abstract feminine noun; use mucha.
drugs | Hablan de muchas drogas. | They talk about many drugs. | Feminine plural noun.
concert | Voy al concierto esta noche. | I am going to the concert tonight. | Masculine noun; a + el = al.
ticket | El boleto cuesta veinte dólares. | The ticket costs twenty dollars. | Masculine noun.
ticket / admission | La entrada es cara. | The admission ticket is expensive. | Feminine noun; often for admission.
dance club | Vamos a la discoteca. | We are going to the dance club. | Feminine noun.
zoo | El zoológico está lejos. | The zoo is far away. | Masculine noun; accent matters.
to entertain | Los artistas entretienen al público. | The artists entertain the audience. | Regular -er verb.
cafeteria | Voy a la cafetería con amigos. | I go to the cafeteria with friends. | Feminine noun; accent matters.
movie theater / cinema | Prefiero ir al cine. | I prefer to go to the movies. | Masculine noun.
movie | La película es dramática. | The movie is dramatic. | Feminine noun.
festival | El festival empieza a las ocho. | The festival starts at eight. | Masculine noun.
to start / begin | El concierto empieza a las siete. | The concert starts at seven. | Stem-changing e → ie.
to return | Vuelvo a casa tarde. | I return home late. | Stem-changing o → ue.
to think | Pienso que el rock es mejor. | I think rock is better. | Stem-changing e → ie.
to want / to love a person | Quiero viajar. | I want to travel. | Stem-changing e → ie.
to sleep | Duermo mucho los fines de semana. | I sleep a lot on weekends. | Stem-changing o → ue.
to ask for / request | Pido ayuda en el hotel. | I ask for help at the hotel. | Stem-changing e → i.
to say / tell | Yo digo la verdad. | I tell the truth. | Irregular yo form: digo.
to repeat | Repito la pregunta. | I repeat the question. | Stem-changing e → i.
to follow / continue | Sigo las recomendaciones. | I follow the recommendations. | Stem-changing e → i; yo sigo.
to remember | Recuerdo mis vacaciones. | I remember my vacation. | Stem-changing o → ue.
to lose | Pierdo las llaves del hotel. | I lose the hotel keys. | Stem-changing e → ie.
to find | Encuentro mi pasaporte. | I find my passport. | Stem-changing o → ue.
to get / obtain | Consigo información sobre cine. | I get information about movies. | Stem-changing e → i.
recommendation | Sigo la recomendación del crítico. | I follow the critic's recommendation. | Feminine noun; accent matters.
critic | El crítico dice que es buena. | The critic says it is good. | Masculine noun; feminine is la crítica.
documentary | El documental muestra una historia real. | The documentary shows a real story. | Masculine noun.
story / history | La historia es interesante. | The story is interesting. | Feminine noun.
action | Prefiero las películas de acción. | I prefer action movies. | Feminine noun; accent matters.
horror | No me gustan las películas de horror. | I do not like horror movies. | Masculine noun.
rock music | El rock me gusta mucho. | I like rock music a lot. | Masculine noun.
rap music | El rap es popular. | Rap is popular. | Masculine noun.
vacation | Las vacaciones son en julio. | Vacation is in July. | Usually plural in Spanish.

hotel vocabulary set: la habitación, la cama, la llave, el piso, la planta baja, el/la huésped | La habitación está en el segundo piso. | The room is on the second floor. | Good compression card for related hotel nouns.
room | La habitación doble tiene dos camas. | The double room has two beds. | Feminine noun.
bed | La cama está cómoda. | The bed is comfortable. | Feminine noun; often with estar.
key | Tengo la llave del hotel. | I have the hotel key. | Feminine noun.
floor | Mi habitación está en el tercer piso. | My room is on the third floor. | Masculine noun.
ground floor | La recepción está en la planta baja. | The reception is on the ground floor. | Fixed phrase.
to confirm a reservation | Necesito confirmar una reservación. | I need to confirm a reservation. | Common travel phrase.
guest | El huésped llega hoy. | The guest arrives today. | Can be masculine or feminine.
luggage | Llevo mucho equipaje. | I carry a lot of luggage. | Mass noun; use mucho, not muchos.

transportation set: el aeropuerto, el avión, la estación de autobuses, la estación del tren, la estación del metro, la llegada, la salida, el pasaje | La salida es a las seis. | The departure is at six. | Compression card for related travel nouns.
airport | El aeropuerto está lejos del hotel. | The airport is far from the hotel. | Masculine noun.
airplane | El avión sale temprano. | The airplane leaves early. | Masculine noun; accent matters.
bus station | La estación de autobuses está cerca. | The bus station is nearby. | Feminine noun phrase.

cards.term: la estación del tren / del metro
cards.definition: train station / metro station | La estación del metro está en el centro. | The metro station is downtown. | Estación can mean station or season depending on context.
customs officer | El inspector de aduanas revisa mi pasaporte. | The customs officer checks my passport. | Travel phrase.
arrival / departure | La llegada es a las ocho y la salida es a las seis. | The arrival is at eight and the departure is at six. | Common travel pair.

cards.term: el pasaje / de ida y vuelta
cards.definition: ticket / round trip | Compro un pasaje de ida y vuelta. | I buy a round-trip ticket. | Fixed travel phrase.

nature set: la playa, el mar, el campo, el paisaje, la pesca, pescar, acampar | Prefiero el campo por el silencio. | I prefer the countryside because of the silence. | Compression card for nature vocabulary.
beach | La playa es muy bonita. | The beach is very beautiful. | Feminine noun.
sea / ocean | El mar está tranquilo. | The sea is calm. | Masculine noun.
countryside | Prefiero el campo. | I prefer the countryside. | Masculine noun.
landscape | El paisaje es hermoso. | The landscape is beautiful. | Masculine noun.
fishing / to fish | Me gusta pescar en el mar. | I like to fish in the sea. | Noun + verb pair.
to camp | Vamos a acampar en verano. | We are going camping in the summer. | Regular -ar verb.

activities set: hacer las maletas, tomar fotos, sacar fotos, ir de compras, montar a caballo, hacer surf | Me gusta tomar fotos y hacer surf. | I like to take pictures and surf. | Compression card for common vacation actions.
to pack luggage | Hago las maletas antes del viaje. | I pack before the trip. | Fixed phrase; hacer is irregular in yo form.
to take pictures | Tomamos fotos en la playa. | We take pictures at the beach. | Both forms are common.
to go shopping | Me gusta ir de compras en vacaciones. | I like to go shopping on vacation. | Fixed expression.
to go horseback riding | Quiero montar a caballo en el campo. | I want to go horseback riding in the countryside. | Fixed phrase.
to surf | Hacemos surf en la playa. | We surf at the beach. | Common borrowed phrase with hacer.

weather set: hace frío, hace calor, llueve, está lloviendo, está nublado, está soleado, hace buen tiempo, hace fresco, va a llover, nieva | Hoy hace fresco y está nublado. | Today it is cool and cloudy. | Good summary card for all major weather phrases.
weather / time | ¿Qué tiempo hace hoy? | What is the weather like today? | Means weather or time depending on context.
it is cold / it is hot | Hace calor en julio. | It is hot in July. | Weather uses hacer, not estar.
it rains / it is raining | Está lloviendo ahora. | It is raining now. | Llueve is general; está lloviendo is happening now.
cloudy / sunny | Está soleado hoy. | It is sunny today. | Usually used with estar.
the weather is nice / it is cool | Hace buen tiempo en primavera. | The weather is nice in spring. | Fixed weather expressions.
it is going to rain / it snows | Creo que va a llover mañana. | I think it is going to rain tomorrow. | Near future uses ir + a + infinitive.
seasons set: el invierno, la primavera, el verano, el otoño, el mes | Mi estación favorita es el verano. | My favorite season is summer. | Good compressed season card.

cards.term: el invierno / la primavera / el verano / el otoño
cards.definition: winter / spring / summer / fall | El invierno es frío y la primavera es bonita. | Winter is cold and spring is beautiful. | Seasons are common class vocab.
month | Enero es el primer mes del año. | January is the first month of the year. | Masculine noun.
January / July | Voy a la playa en julio. | I go to the beach in July. | Months are lowercase in Spanish.

estar adjectives set: cómodo/a, enamorado/a, cerrado/a, ocupado/a, equivocado/a, alegre, confundido/a, nervioso/a, enojado/a, limpio/a, sucio/a, cansado/a, contento/a, avergonzado/a | Estoy nervioso por el examen. | I am nervous about the exam. | Use estar, not ser, for temporary states and conditions.
comfortable | La habitación está cómoda. | The room is comfortable. | Usually with estar.
in love | Estoy enamorado de Ana. | I am in love with Ana. | Use estar for this emotional state.
closed / clean / dirty | La biblioteca está cerrada y mi cuarto está sucio. | The library is closed and my room is dirty. | Physical conditions usually use estar.
busy / wrong, mistaken | Estamos ocupados y tú estás equivocado. | We are busy and you are wrong. | Temporary conditions with estar.
happy / confused / nervous / angry | Ella está alegre pero yo estoy nervioso. | She is happy but I am nervous. | Emotion adjectives often tested with estar.
tired / happy / embarrassed | Estoy cansado pero contento. | I am tired but happy. | Temporary feelings use estar.
very | Estoy muy cansado. | I am very tired. | Use before adjectives or adverbs.
a lot / many | Hay muchas personas y tengo mucha tarea. | There are many people and I have a lot of homework. | Must agree with nouns; mucho can also modify verbs.

cards.term: la persona / el gimnasio / la tarea
cards.definition: person / gym / homework | Hay muchas personas en el gimnasio y tengo mucha tarea. | There are many people in the gym and I have a lot of homework. | Persona is feminine even if referring to a man.
¿Dónde?, ¿Cuándo?, ¿Qué?, ¿Cómo?, ¿Con quién? | ¿Dónde vas y con quién? | Where are you going and with whom? | Question words take accent marks.
to be going to do something | Voy a estudiar en la biblioteca. | I am going to study in the library. | Near-future structure that appears often in conversation.
